Run Simulations and Bootstrap Community Metrics Across Interaction Weights

Source: R/run_sim_and_bootstrap_multiple_weights.R
run_sim_and_bootstrap_multiple_weights.Rd

This function automates the process of running the fig wasp community simulator with multiple values of interaction_weight, and performs bootstrap resampling on each result to assess variability in community metrics.

Usage

run_sim_and_bootstrap_multiple_weights(
  weights = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9),
  n_reps = 500,
  sample_size = 200,
  seed = 42
)

Arguments

weights

A numeric vector of interaction_weight values to evaluate. Default: c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9).

n_reps

Number of bootstrap replicates per weight. Default: 500.

sample_size

Number of figs to sample per replicate. Default: 200.

seed

Random seed for reproducibility. Default: 42.

Value

A named list of data frames. Each element corresponds to one interaction weight and contains bootstrap resampled community metrics computed from the simulation.

Details

This function requires that global objects such as lhs_best_param_list, observed_interaction_matrix, and simulate_figwasp_community() be pre-defined. The function uses resample_metrics_from_simulation() internally to compute alpha diversity or other community structure metrics for each simulation run.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
results_list <- run_sim_and_bootstrap_multiple_weights(
  weights = seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.2),
  n_reps = 300,
  sample_size = 150
)
} # }
